Endangerment_Platform-Generated.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Endangerment, placing it alongside familiar child-safety or self-harm cases that presume a human speaker or uploader.
Endangerment_Platform-Generated.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Platform-Generated surfaces that the proximate producer of harm is the platform's own conversational or generative layer, which ordinary user enforcement does not reach.

Dangerous Misinformation_Platform-Genera.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Dangerous Misinformation, placing it alongside false election, health, or crisis information shared by ordinary users.
Dangerous Misinformation_Platform-Genera.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Platform-Generated shows that the misleading content originated in the platform's own assistant or answer layer, not in user speech alone.

Dangerous Misinformation_Recommender-Amp.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Dangerous Misinformation, placing it near other synthetic-media or misleading-context cases.
Dangerous Misinformation_Recommender-Amp.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Recommender-Amplified surfaces that the principal risk comes from scale and visibility decisions, not just from the original creation of the content.

Hateful Content_Hybrid-Agency.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Hateful Content, placing it alongside racist or discriminatory content posted by users or advertisers.
Hateful Content_Hybrid-Agency.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Hybrid-Agency reveals split authorship: user-supplied inputs or assets are transformed by a platform generation system into the final harmful output.

Endangerment_Hybrid-Agency.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Endangerment, grouping it with cases where a user directly encourages unsafe behavior.
Endangerment_Hybrid-Agency.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Hybrid-Agency clarifies that the unsafe output emerged from an interaction between user prompting and platform generation rather than from either side alone.

Privacy Violation_Design-Layer.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Privacy Violation, placing it near doxxing or disclosure cases handled through content takedowns.
Privacy Violation_Design-Layer.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Design-Layer surfaces that the platform's interface, defaults, or disclosure architecture enabled the violation before any specific abusive post appeared.

Impersonation_User-Assisted-by-AI.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Impersonation, placing it alongside fake-account and identity-fraud cases.
Impersonation_User-Assisted-by-AI.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding User-Assisted-by-AI shows that a human actor remains central, but AI materially lowers the cost and raises the scale or realism of the deception.

Fraud_User-Assisted-by-AI.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Fraud, placing it near scams and confidence tricks that target users for money or sensitive information.
Fraud_User-Assisted-by-AI.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding User-Assisted-by-AI makes visible how AI augments persuasion, targeting, or message production even when a user remains the principal wrongdoer.

Sexualized Abuse_Platform-Generated.harmOnly: Under harm-axis coding alone, this event would be classified as Sexualized Abuse, grouping it with explicit or exploitative content created by users.
Sexualized Abuse_Platform-Generated.actorInsight: Adding actor-axis coding Platform-Generated surfaces that the platform's own model behavior is generating the abusive material, shifting the policy question toward system design and deployment governance.
